title: Privacy information regarding third-party services
meta-description: The following table lists the third-party services currently used by CMS in conjunction with HealthCare.gov and provides links to the privacy policies for each third-party service provider. Also, the table provides links to the instructions for opting out of each service and a link where you can learn more about each service by reviewing the CMS Third-party Website Privacy Impact Assessment.
bite: HealthCare.gov uses third-party services. Get a list of third-party service providers, discover how to opt out, and learn more. Official U.S. government site.
excerpt: HealthCare.gov uses third-party services. Get a list of third-party service providers, discover how to opt out, and learn more. Official U.S. government site.
content:
CMS, including HealthCare.gov, uses these third-party services. For each third-party service provider we work with, you can access the privacy policies, get information on how to opt out of the service, and learn more about their services.

Category: Advertising tools

Third-party toolPurpose and useLink to third-party’s privacy policyHow to opt out of this serviceLink to the CMS "Third-party Website and Application Privacy Impact Assessment"
AdsmovilDelivers behaviorally targeted ads to desired consumers. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.Adsmovil privacy policy

Contact Adsmovil

CMS Privacy Settings

Adsmovil privacy impact assessment (PDF, 62 KB)
Adobe Ad CloudServes display and retargeting ads to people who have visited HealthCare.gov. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.Adobe privacy policy

Adobe

CMS Privacy Settings

AdChoices

 

Adobe Ad Cloud privacy impact assessment (PDF, 19.8 KB)
AOLServes digital ads on third-party and AOL sites for both new users and to consumers who have visited HealthCare.gov. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.AOL/Yahoo privacy policyAOL/Oath privacy policy highlightsAOL privacy impact assessment (PDF, 155 KB)
BINGServes digital ads on pages that display Bing search results. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.Microsoft privacy statementMicrosoft ad settingsBING privacy impact assessment (PDF, 106 KB)
FacebookCMS uses Facebook Ads to deploy digital display ads and video ads across the Facebook platform to consumers. Facebook is a free social networking site that allows Facebook registered users to create profiles, upload photos and videos, send messages, and keep in touch with the people in their social network.Facebook privacy basicsMembers can choose to opt out of tracking on third party sites by adjusting their advertising preferences in their Facebook account. Facebook honors all “Limit Ad Tracking” settings on mobile devices. On Facebook, all ads give users the ability to: Hide this ad. If a user chooses this, the user won't get the ad again. This is specific to the ad ID within the campaign only or Hide all ads.

CMS Privacy Settings

AdChoices		Facebook Ads privacy impact assessment
Google Advertising ServicesServes digital ads on third-party and Google sites for both new users and consumers who have visited HealthCare.gov. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.Google privacy policy

Google ad personalization

CMS Privacy Settings

Google Advertising Services privacy impact assessment (PDF, 89 KB)
Huddled MassesDelivers behaviorally targeted ads to desired consumers. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.Direct Digital Holding privacy policyCMS Privacy SettingsHuddled Masses privacy impact assessment  (PDF, 105 KB)
HuluDelivers behaviorally targeted video ads to desired consumers. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising. Hulu privacy policy

Hulu opt-out choices

CMS Privacy Settings

Hulu privacy impact assessment (PDF, 19 KB)
Integral Ad Science (IAS)Viewability/verification tool that ensures ads aren't shown in front of non-human traffic (i.e. bots), nor next to non-brand safe content including, but not limited to, violent or graphic material. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.IAS website privacy policy

IAS website privacy policy

CMS Privacy Settings

IAS privacy impact assessment
LinkedInCMS uses LinkedIn Marketing Solutions to deploy digital display ads and video ads across the LinkedIn platform to consumers. LinkedIn is a social networking site designed to allow registered members to establish and document networks of people they know and trust professionally.LinkedIn privacy policyMembers can choose to opt out of tracking on third party sites by adjusting their advertising preferences in their LinkedIn account. This is the most persistent method of opting out. LinkedIn provides links to industry opt-out via its Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy and Help Center. These industry opt-out methods can be independently accessed outside of LinkedIn.

CMS Privacy Settings

AdChoices		LinkedIn Advertising Services privacy impact assessment

MediaMath privacy policy

PandoraPandora Internet Radio (also known as Pandora Radio or simply Pandora) is a music streaming and automated music recommendation service. Pandora provides a free, advertising supported version of its services. Advertising on Pandora can include audio, video, and visual advertisements.Pandora privacy policy
Pandora opt-out choices

PulsePointPulsePoint is a platform that shares ads on mobile applications and websites. CMS uses PulsePoint to deliver behaviorally targeted ads to desired consumers. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising.PulsePoint Private Policy

ResonateCMS uses Resonate to deliver behaviorally targeted advertising on third party websites to encourage consumers to visit HealthCare.gov. In addition, Resonate also delivers retargeted advertising to consumers who previously visited HealthCare.gov to encourage them to return to HealthCare.gov.Resonate privacy policyResonate opt-out choices

CMS Privacy Settings

TremorTremor delivers behaviorally targeted digital ads based on automatic content recognition TV retargeting. Uses web beacons and persistent cookies for digital advertising. Tremor privacy policy

Tremor privacy impact assessment (PDF, 80 KB)
TwitchTwitch is an online video sharing platform that allows consumers to view, upload, and share video content, often related to video gaming. The platform also allows users to broadcast live-streamed content related to video games, such as commentary at video game conventions or announcing at competitive events. Advertising on Twitch can include video, display, and native products.Twitch privacy notice Twitch opt-out choices

CMS Privacy Settings

AdChoices		Twitch Advertising privacy impact assessment
ViantServes targeted ads to desired consumers, and identifies consumers interactions with advertisements. Uses web beacons, pixels and persistent cookies for digital advertising.Viant privacy policy (PDF, 159 KB)

New RelicMonitors and evaluates the web and system transactions of HealthCare.gov to assist with troubleshooting. Uses session cookies.New Relic privacy policyNew Relic opt-out choicesNew Relic privacy impact assessment (PDF, 71 KB)

Category: Social media

Third-party toolPurpose and useLink to third-party’s privacy policyHow to opt out of this serviceLink to the CMS "Third-party Website and Application Privacy Impact Assessment"
FacebookCMS created a HealthCare.gov Facebook page for educational purposes. The page allows for consumers to ask questions and to communicate concerns. Consumers may like the page, or interact with the educational content by commenting, replying, or sharing.Facebook privacy policy Facebook privacy impact assessment
InstagramCMS created a HealthCare.gov Instagram page for educational purposes. The page allows consumers to ask questions and communicate concerns. The page allows consumers to like, share, comment, and reply to educational content.Instagram privacy policyCMS Privacy Settings

AdChoices		Instagram privacy impact assessment (PDF, 69 KB)
LinkedInCMS uses LinkedIn Marketing Solutions to deploy digital display ads and video ads across the LinkedIn platform to consumers. LinkedIn is a social networking site designed to allow registered members to establish and document networks of people they know and trust professionally.LinkedIn Privacy Policy

Members can choose to opt out of tracking on third party sites by adjusting their advertising preferences in their LinkedIn account. This is the most persistent method of opting out. LinkedIn provides links to industry opt-out methods (i.e. the DAA, CDAA, EDAA and NAI opt-out tools) via its Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy and Help Center. These industry opt-out methods can be independently accessed outside of LinkedIn.

CMS Privacy Settings

AdChoices

LinkedIn Advertising Services privacy impact assessment
X, formerly TwitterCMS created a Healthcare.gov X handle for educational purposes. The page allows consumers to ask questions and communicate concerns. Consumers may follow HealthCare.gov or interact with the educational content by tweeting, retweeting, or favoriting tweets.

X privacy policy

X Privacy Settings

CMS Privacy Settings

X privacy impact assessment (PDF, 27 KB)
YouTubeCMS created a HealthCare.gov YouTube page for educational purposes. The page allows consumers to play educational videos. The page allows consumers to “thumbs-up”, share, and comment on videos.

YouTube privacy policy

Category: Privacy Settings 

Third-party toolPurpose and useLink to third-party’s privacy policyHow to opt out of this serviceLink to the CMS "Third-party Website and Application Privacy Impact Assessment"
TealiumCMS uses Tealium as a solution for staff to manage HealthCare.gov tags from a single interface. Specifically, the tool allows CMS to control which third-party tools are active/inactive. Through its Privacy Settings, Tealium also allows consumers to choose which types of third-party tools are active/inactive during their visit.Tealium privacy policyCMS Privacy SettingsTealium privacy impact assessment (PDF, 16 KB)
